question_text: A book of mass 2 kg is placed on a table. A student pushes the book horizontally with a force of 5 N to the right, and friction acts on the book with a force of 8 N to the left. Another student pulls the book with a rope at an angle, applying a force of 6 N horizontally to the right. What is the resultant force acting on the book, and in which direction does it act?
explanation: First, identify all horizontal forces: push = 5 N right, pull = 6 N right, friction = 8 N left. Add forces in the same direction: 5 + 6 = 11 N right. Subtract friction (opposite direction): 11 - 8 = 3 N net force. Since friction (8 N left) is greater than the combined pushes and pulls (11 N right), the resultant force is 3 N to the left.
